item.title: Netflix debuts new 'Playground' gaming app for kids
item.description: Netflix doubled down on its gaming efforts on Monday, launching a new app called "Netflix Playground" that would feature games built ​around popular children's characters such as Peppa Pig and Sesame Street.

item.title: Maker of Stanley tumblers prevails in lawsuit over lead scare
item.description: A federal ​judge dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing the maker of Stanley tumblers ‌of concealing that the popular water bottles contained lead.

item.title: Trump proposes to cut 9,400 TSA workers, $1.5 billion from budget
item.description: The White House is proposing to ​cut more than 9,400 ‌workers and just over $1.5 billion from the 60,000-employee Transportation ​Security Administration that ​handles airport security operations, according ⁠to budget documents.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee, Hammack say inflation is flashing 'orange,' or worse
item.description: Cleveland Federal Reserve President Beth Hammack and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee both see inflation as a far bigger problem than employment, underscoring their support for tighter rather than looser monetary policy ​as the Iran war puts upward pressure on energy prices and the job market remains ‌stuck in low gear.

item.title: FAA proposes to hire 2,300 air traffic controllers in budget request
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said Monday ​it is proposing ‌to hire 2,300 air traffic controller trainees ​as it works ​to address a persistent ⁠lack of personnel.

item.title: White House says increased productivity means Fed can cut rates
item.description: White House economic ‌adviser Kevin Hassett told CNBC on Monday that he believes a "supply ​shock" in the U.S. ​economy caused by capital spending ⁠and higher productivity from artificial intelligence will ​allow the Federal Reserve to ​lower interest rates.

item.title: NY Fed says supply chain pressures heated up in March
item.description: Supply chain pressures heated up in March to ​levels last seen at the ‌start of 2023, data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York said ​Monday.

item.title: Treasury designates BNY as financial agent for Trump accounts
item.description: The ​Bank of New York ‌Mellon Corporation has been chose as a financial agent of the ​U.S. government to support implementation ​of the new Trump Accounts program, ⁠the U.S. Treasury Department ​said on Monday.

item.title: Brazil's Azul appoints ex-Embraer CFO Garcia in finance reshuffle
item.description: Brazilian airline Azul said ‌on Monday that Chief Financial Officer Alex ​Malfitani will step ​down on April 20, with Embraer's ⁠Antonio Carlos Garcia set ​to assume the role.

item.title: US service sector cools in March; price paid measure highest in 3-1/2 years
item.description: U.S. services sector growth slowed in March, while prices paid by ‌businesses for inputs climbed to near a 3-1/2-year high, an early sign that the prolonged war with Iran was boosting inflation pressures.

item.title: New Jersey cannot regulate Kalshi's prediction market, US appeals court rules
item.description: A federal appeals court on ​Monday ruled that New ‌Jersey gaming regulators cannot prevent Kalshi from ​allowing people ​in the state to use ⁠its prediction ​market to place financial ​bets on the outcome of sporting events.

item.title: Barings' private credit fund limits withdrawals after redemption requests surge
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Barings capped withdrawals at 5% of shares after a ​surge in redemption requests, the latest in a series of similar ‌moves by asset managers in recent months.

item.title: BlackRock files for Nasdaq-100 fund, expanding competition with Invesco
item.description: BlackRock has filed for an exchange-traded fund that will track the Nasdaq-100 , in a challenge ​to Invesco's dominance in a market where ‌only a handful of funds directly follow the tech-heavy index.

item.title: Brazil's Embraer CFO Garcia resigns, CEO Gomes Neto to take interim role
item.description: Brazilian ‌planemaker Embraer ​said ​on Monday that ⁠CFO ​Antonio ​Carlos Garcia has ​resigned ​and CEO Francisco ‌Gomes ⁠Neto will assume ​the ​position ⁠on ​an ​interim ⁠basis.

item.title: Investors press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on water, power use in US data centers
item.description: Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies ​are coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects.

item.title: National Healthcare Properties moves closer to public markets with US IPO filing
item.description: Real estate investment trust ​National Healthcare Properties on ‌Monday made public its paperwork for an ​initial public offering ​in the United States.

item.title: Oracle appoints Hilary Maxson as CFO
item.description: Oracle said ​on Monday it ‌had appointed Hilary Maxson as chief financial officer, effective ​immediately.

item.title: US equity funds see a second successive weekly inflow
item.description: U.S. equity funds witnessed substantial inflows in the seven days to April 1 as worries ​over the Middle East war eased temporarily after ‌President Donald Trump indicated that the United States was nearing the completion of its objectives for the war.

item.title: Goldman Sachs private credit fund defies redemption surge across industry
item.description: Goldman Sachs' private credit fund said on Monday investors requested to repurchase just under 5% of shares in the first quarter, staying within its cap and defying a broader rise in redemption requests across the ​sector.

item.title: Neurocrine to buy Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion
item.description: Neurocrine Biosciences will acquire rare disease drugmaker ​Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion in ‌cash, the companies said on Monday, marking the neuroscience-focused drugmaker's expansion into ​metabolic disorders.

item.title: Madison Air targets $13.2 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Ventilation and ‌filtration systems provider Madison Air Solutions said on ​Monday it was ​targeting a valuation of ⁠up to $13.2 billion ​in its initial public ​offering in the United States.

item.title: JPMorgan's Dimon warns Iran war may drive inflation and interest rates higher
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned on Monday that the war in Iran risks oil and commodity ​price shocks that could keep inflation sticky and push interest rates higher than the market now expects.

item.title: US crude premiums climb to record levels as Asia, Europe compete for supply
item.description: Spot premiums for U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude have jumped ​to all-time highs as competition between Asian and European refiners for supply heats up ‌to replace Middle Eastern oil flows disrupted by the Iran war, industry sources said.

item.title: U.S. auto safety regulator closes probe into Tesla's driver assistance feature
item.description: The National ​Highway ‌Traffic Safety ​Administration ​said on Monday ⁠that ​it ​had closed a ​probe ​into Tesla vehicles' "Actually ‌Smart ⁠Summon" feature, covering ​roughly ​2.59 ⁠million ​vehicles.

item.title: US stock futures edge up as investors assess Mideast ceasefire prospects
item.description: U.S. stock index futures inched ​higher on Monday, after the main indexes marked their biggest weekly jump in four ‌months in the last session, as investors assessed prospects of an end to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Global equity funds draw second weekly inflow amid war de-escalation hopes
item.description: Investors bought global equity funds worth a net $15.02 billion in the March 26-April 1 period, logging a second ​straight week of inflows, on hopes that the U.S.-Israeli war ‌with Iran could soon de-escalate.

item.title: Indian IT firms face subdued fourth quarter as war, AI concerns persist; weak rupee helps earnings
item.description: Top Indian information technology firms are set to report another lacklustre quarter, with revenue and profit seen rising around 10% year-on-year largely on a weaker rupee rather than underlying growth, seven ​brokerages said.

item.title: Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March
item.description: Tesla saw ​its car registrations ‌in South Korea rise ​330% ​to 11,134 vehicles in ⁠March ​from year earlier, ​market researcher Carisyou said on ​Monday.

item.title: Citigroup pushes back Fed rate cut timeline after strong job numbers
item.description: Citigroup has pushed back its Fed rate-cut timeline, citing unexpectedly strong U.S. ​job gains and persistent inflation risks.

item.title: India's Wipro rises after deal to acquire Olam Group’s IT business
item.description: Shares of Wipro rose as much as 3.2% on Monday after the Indian IT ​services firm agreed to buy the IT services business ‌of Singapore-based Olam Group for an enterprise value of $375 million.
